2026-04-06T11:22:16.7188618Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7189201Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7189644Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7190022Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7190339Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7190587Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7190752Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7190941Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7191124Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7191365Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7191578Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7191871Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-06T11:22:16.7192140Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

client is instantiated and root directory client is consumed from it, you\\u0026#39;re not allowed to use SetHttpHeadersAsync on that root directory.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nSetHttpHeadersAsync on root directory client should act normally.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nSetHttpHeadersAsync throws 404 whenever the root directory client is used.\\n\\n### the perspective of a developer writing unit tests, that I can not (easily) construct many of the model types in the SDK.\\n\\nNoting that:\\n- Some models have internal constructors (e.g., `BlobDownloadResult`)\\n- Most models have internal setters\\n- Model properties are not declared as virtual\\n\\nConsider `BlobDownloadDetails` as an example.
